rationale: Both candidates converge on the same core conclusion the ground-truth inline comment makes (removing already_submitted is correct because both submit paths reject list prompts), but Y grounds it more precisely by citing the actual StagePool rejection sites (stage_pool.py:951-955, 1022-1026) matching the ground truth's own framing, while X approximates locations ('line ~1301/~1296') that appear to misattribute where the rejection logic actually lives. Y also correctly recommends APPROVE, mirroring the real reviewers' LGTM outcome, whereas X's 'major/FOLLOW-UP REQUIRED' framing for a missing unit test somewhat overstates severity relative to actual reviewer consensus. X compensates with more prescriptive, code-level actionable suggestions (exact test to add), giving it a slight edge in actionability, but Y's overall grounding and accuracy give it the edge.
